members.name: GetFeature
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the specified [param feature] is enabled.

members.name: GetFlag
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the specified [param flag] is enabled.

members.name: GetTexture
members.summary: Returns the [Texture2D] associated with the specified texture [param param].

members.name: SetFeature
members.signature: public void SetFeature(int feature, bool enable)
members.summary: If [param enable] is [code]true[/code], enables the specified [param feature]. Many features that are available in [BaseMaterial3D] need to be enabled before use. This way, the cost for using the feature is only incurred when specified. Features can also be enabled by setting their corresponding property to [code]true[/code].

members.name: SetFlag
members.signature: public void SetFlag(int flag, bool enable)
members.summary: If [param enable] is [code]true[/code], enables the specified [param flag]. Flags are optional behavior that can be turned on and off. Only one flag can be enabled at a time with this function, the flag enumerators cannot be bit-masked together to enable or disable multiple flags at once. Flags can also be enabled by setting their corresponding property to [code]true[/code].

members.name: SetTexture
members.signature: public void SetTexture(int param, Texture2D texture)
members.summary: Sets the texture for the slot specified by [param param].

members.name: AlbedoColor
members.summary: The material's base color. [b]Note:[/b] If [member detail_enabled] is [code]true[/code] and a [member detail_albedo] texture is specified, [member albedo_color] will [i]not[/i] modulate the detail texture. This can be used to color partial areas of a material by not specifying an albedo texture and using a transparent [member detail_albedo] texture instead.

members.name: AlbedoTexture
members.summary: Texture to multiply by [member albedo_color]. Used for basic texturing of objects. If the texture appears unexpectedly too dark or too bright, check [member albedo_texture_force_srgb].

members.name: AlbedoTextureForceSrgb
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], forces a conversion of the [member albedo_texture] from nonlinear sRGB encoding to linear encoding. See also [member vertex_color_is_srgb]. This should only be enabled when needed (typically when using a [ViewportTexture] as [member albedo_texture]). If [member albedo_texture_force_srgb] is [code]true[/code] when it shouldn't be, the texture will appear to be too dark. If [member albedo_texture_force_srgb] is [code]false[/code] when it shouldn't be, the texture will appear to be too bright.

members.name: AlbedoTextureMsdf
members.summary: Enables multichannel signed distance field rendering shader. Use [member msdf_pixel_range] and [member msdf_outline_size] to configure MSDF parameters.

members.name: AlphaAntialiasingEdge
members.summary: Threshold at which antialiasing will be applied on the alpha channel.

members.name: AlphaAntialiasingMode
members.summary: The type of alpha antialiasing to apply.

members.name: AlphaHashScale
members.summary: The hashing scale for Alpha Hash. Recommended values between [code]0[/code] and [code]2[/code].

members.name: AlphaScissorThreshold
members.summary: Threshold at which the alpha scissor will discard values. Higher values will result in more pixels being discarded. If the material becomes too opaque at a distance, try increasing [member alpha_scissor_threshold]. If the material disappears at a distance, try decreasing [member alpha_scissor_threshold].

members.name: Anisotropy
members.summary: The strength of the anisotropy effect. This is multiplied by [member anisotropy_flowmap]'s alpha channel if a texture is defined there and the texture contains an alpha channel.

members.name: AnisotropyEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], anisotropy is enabled. Anisotropy changes the shape of the specular blob and aligns it to tangent space. This is useful for brushed aluminum and hair reflections. [b]Note:[/b] Mesh tangents are needed for anisotropy to work. If the mesh does not contain tangents, the anisotropy effect will appear broken. [b]Note:[/b] Material anisotropy should not to be confused with anisotropic texture filtering, which can be enabled by setting [member texture_filter] to [constant TEXTURE_FILTER_LINEAR_WITH_MIPMAPS_ANISOTROPIC].

members.name: AnisotropyFlowmap
members.summary: Texture that offsets the tangent map for anisotropy calculations and optionally controls the anisotropy effect (if an alpha channel is present). The flowmap texture is expected to be a derivative map, with the red channel representing distortion on the X axis and green channel representing distortion on the Y axis. Values below 0.5 will result in negative distortion, whereas values above 0.5 will result in positive distortion. If present, the texture's alpha channel will be used to multiply the strength of the [member anisotropy] effect. Fully opaque pixels will keep the anisotropy effect's original strength while fully transparent pixels will disable the anisotropy effect entirely. The flowmap texture's blue channel is ignored.

members.name: AoEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], ambient occlusion is enabled. Ambient occlusion darkens areas based on the [member ao_texture].

members.name: AoLightAffect
members.summary: Amount that ambient occlusion affects lighting from lights. If [code]0[/code], ambient occlusion only affects ambient light. If [code]1[/code], ambient occlusion affects lights just as much as it affects ambient light. This can be used to impact the strength of the ambient occlusion effect, but typically looks unrealistic.

members.name: AoOnUv2
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], use [code]UV2[/code] coordinates to look up from the [member ao_texture].

members.name: AoTexture
members.summary: Texture that defines the amount of ambient occlusion for a given point on the object.

members.name: AoTextureChannel
members.summary: Specifies the channel of the [member ao_texture] in which the ambient occlusion information is stored. This is useful when you store the information for multiple effects in a single texture. For example if you stored metallic in the red channel, roughness in the blue, and ambient occlusion in the green you could reduce the number of textures you use.

members.name: Backlight
members.summary: The color used by the backlight effect. Represents the light passing through an object.

members.name: BacklightEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the backlight effect is enabled. See also [member subsurf_scatter_transmittance_enabled].

members.name: BacklightTexture
members.summary: Texture used to control the backlight effect per-pixel. Added to [member backlight].

members.name: BentNormalEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the bent normal map is enabled. This allows for more accurate indirect lighting and specular occlusion.

members.name: BentNormalTexture
members.summary: Texture that specifies the average direction of incoming ambient light at a given pixel. The [member bent_normal_texture] only uses the red and green channels; the blue and alpha channels are ignored. The normal read from [member bent_normal_texture] is oriented around the surface normal provided by the [Mesh]. [b]Note:[/b] A bent normal map is different from a regular normal map. When baking a bent normal map make sure to use [b]a cosine distribution[/b] for the bent normal map to work correctly. [b]Note:[/b] The mesh must have both normals and tangents defined in its vertex data. Otherwise, the shading produced by the bent normal map will not look correct. If creating geometry with [SurfaceTool], you can use [method SurfaceTool.generate_normals] and [method SurfaceTool.generate_tangents] to automatically generate normals and tangents respectively. [b]Note:[/b] Godot expects the bent normal map to use X+, Y+, and Z+ coordinates. See [url=http://wiki.polycount.com/wiki/Normal_Map_Technical_Details#Common_Swizzle_Coordinates]this page[/url] for a comparison of normal map coordinates expected by popular engines.

members.name: BillboardKeepScale
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the shader will keep the scale set for the mesh. Otherwise, the scale is lost when billboarding. Only applies when [member billboard_mode] is not [constant BILLBOARD_DISABLED].

members.name: BillboardMode
members.summary: Controls how the object faces the camera. [b]Note:[/b] Billboard mode is not suitable for VR because the left-right vector of the camera is not horizontal when the screen is attached to your head instead of on the table. See [url=https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/41567]GitHub issue #41567[/url] for details.

members.name: BlendMode
members.summary: The material's blend mode. [b]Note:[/b] Values other than [code]Mix[/code] force the object into the transparent pipeline.

members.name: Clearcoat
members.summary: Sets the strength of the clearcoat effect. Setting to [code]0[/code] looks the same as disabling the clearcoat effect.

members.name: ClearcoatEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], clearcoat rendering is enabled. Adds a secondary transparent pass to the lighting calculation resulting in an added specular blob. This makes materials appear as if they have a clear layer on them that can be either glossy or rough. [b]Note:[/b] Clearcoat rendering is not visible if the material's [member shading_mode] is [constant SHADING_MODE_UNSHADED].

members.name: ClearcoatRoughness
members.summary: Sets the roughness of the clearcoat pass. A higher value results in a rougher clearcoat while a lower value results in a smoother clearcoat.

members.name: ClearcoatTexture
members.summary: Texture that defines the strength of the clearcoat effect and the glossiness of the clearcoat. Strength is specified in the red channel while glossiness is specified in the green channel.

members.name: CullMode
members.summary: Determines which side of the triangle to cull depending on whether the triangle faces towards or away from the camera.

members.name: DepthDrawMode
members.summary: Determines when depth rendering takes place. See also [member transparency].

members.name: DepthTest
members.summary: Determines which comparison operator is used when testing depth. [b]Note:[/b] Changing [member depth_test] to a non-default value only has a visible effect when used on a transparent material, or a material that has [member depth_draw_mode] set to [constant DEPTH_DRAW_DISABLED].

members.name: DetailAlbedo
members.summary: Texture that specifies the color of the detail overlay. [member detail_albedo]'s alpha channel is used as a mask, even when the material is opaque. To use a dedicated texture as a mask, see [member detail_mask]. [b]Note:[/b] [member detail_albedo] is [i]not[/i] modulated by [member albedo_color].

members.name: DetailBlendMode
members.summary: Specifies how the [member detail_albedo] should blend with the current [code]ALBEDO[/code].

members.name: DetailEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], enables the detail overlay. Detail is a second texture that gets mixed over the surface of the object based on [member detail_mask] and [member detail_albedo]'s alpha channel. This can be used to add variation to objects, or to blend between two different albedo/normal textures.

members.name: DetailMask
members.summary: Texture used to specify how the detail textures get blended with the base textures. [member detail_mask] can be used together with [member detail_albedo]'s alpha channel (if any).

members.name: DetailNormal
members.summary: Texture that specifies the per-pixel normal of the detail overlay. The [member detail_normal] texture only uses the red and green channels; the blue and alpha channels are ignored. The normal read from [member detail_normal] is oriented around the surface normal provided by the [Mesh]. [b]Note:[/b] Godot expects the normal map to use X+, Y+, and Z+ coordinates. See [url=http://wiki.polycount.com/wiki/Normal_Map_Technical_Details#Common_Swizzle_Coordinates]this page[/url] for a comparison of normal map coordinates expected by popular engines.

members.name: DetailUvLayer
members.summary: Specifies whether to use [code]UV[/code] or [code]UV2[/code] for the detail layer.

members.name: DiffuseMode
members.summary: The algorithm used for diffuse light scattering.

members.name: DisableAmbientLight
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the object receives no ambient light.

members.name: DisableFog
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the object will not be affected by fog (neither volumetric nor depth fog). This is useful for unshaded or transparent materials (e.g. particles), which without this setting will be affected even if fully transparent.

members.name: DisableReceiveShadows
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the object receives no shadow that would otherwise be cast onto it.

members.name: DisableSpecularOcclusion
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], disables specular occlusion even if [member ProjectSettings.rendering/reflections/specular_occlusion/enabled] is [code]false[/code].

members.name: DistanceFadeMaxDistance
members.summary: Distance at which the object appears fully opaque. [b]Note:[/b] If [member distance_fade_max_distance] is less than [member distance_fade_min_distance], the behavior will be reversed. The object will start to fade away at [member distance_fade_max_distance] and will fully disappear once it reaches [member distance_fade_min_distance].

members.name: DistanceFadeMinDistance
members.summary: Distance at which the object starts to become visible. If the object is less than this distance away, it will be invisible. [b]Note:[/b] If [member distance_fade_min_distance] is greater than [member distance_fade_max_distance], the behavior will be reversed. The object will start to fade away at [member distance_fade_max_distance] and will fully disappear once it reaches [member distance_fade_min_distance].

members.name: DistanceFadeMode
members.summary: Specifies which type of fade to use. Can be any of the [enum DistanceFadeMode]s.

members.name: Emission
members.summary: The emitted light's color. See [member emission_enabled].

members.name: EmissionEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the body emits light. Emitting light makes the object appear brighter. The object can also cast light on other objects if a [VoxelGI], SDFGI, or [LightmapGI] is used and this object is used in baked lighting.

members.name: EmissionEnergyMultiplier
members.summary: Multiplier for emitted light. See [member emission_enabled].

members.name: EmissionIntensity
members.summary: Luminance of emitted light, measured in nits (candela per square meter). Only available when [member ProjectSettings.rendering/lights_and_shadows/use_physical_light_units] is enabled. The default is roughly equivalent to an indoor lightbulb.

members.name: EmissionOnUv2
members.summary: Use [code]UV2[/code] to read from the [member emission_texture].

members.name: EmissionOperator
members.summary: Sets how [member emission] interacts with [member emission_texture]. Can either add or multiply.

members.name: EmissionTexture
members.summary: Texture that specifies how much surface emits light at a given point.

members.name: FixedSize
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the object is rendered at the same size regardless of distance. The object's size on screen is the same as if the camera was [code]1.0[/code] units away from the object's origin, regardless of the actual distance from the camera. The [Camera3D]'s field of view (or [member Camera3D.size] when in orthogonal/frustum mode) still affects the size the object is drawn at.

members.name: FovOverride
members.summary: Overrides the [Camera3D]'s field of view angle (in degrees). [b]Note:[/b] This behaves as if the field of view is set on a [Camera3D] with [member Camera3D.keep_aspect] set to [constant Camera3D.KEEP_HEIGHT]. Additionally, it may not look correct on a non-perspective camera where the field of view setting is ignored.

members.name: Grow
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], enables the vertex grow setting. This can be used to create mesh-based outlines using a second material pass and its [member cull_mode] set to [constant CULL_FRONT]. See also [member grow_amount]. [b]Note:[/b] Vertex growth cannot create new vertices, which means that visible gaps may occur in sharp corners. This can be alleviated by designing the mesh to use smooth normals exclusively using [url=http://wiki.polycount.com/wiki/Face_weighted_normals]face weighted normals[/url] in the 3D authoring software. In this case, grow will be able to join every outline together, just like in the original mesh.

members.name: GrowAmount
members.summary: Grows object vertices in the direction of their normals. Only effective if [member grow] is [code]true[/code].

members.name: HeightmapDeepParallax
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], uses parallax occlusion mapping to represent depth in the material instead of simple offset mapping (see [member heightmap_enabled]). This results in a more convincing depth effect, but is much more expensive on the GPU. Only enable this on materials where it makes a significant visual difference.

members.name: HeightmapEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], height mapping is enabled (also called "parallax mapping" or "depth mapping"). See also [member normal_enabled]. Height mapping is a demanding feature on the GPU, so it should only be used on materials where it makes a significant visual difference. [b]Note:[/b] Height mapping is not supported if triplanar mapping is used on the same material. The value of [member heightmap_enabled] will be ignored if [member uv1_triplanar] is enabled.

members.name: HeightmapFlipBinormal
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], flips the mesh's binormal vectors when interpreting the height map. If the heightmap effect looks strange when the camera moves (even with a reasonable [member heightmap_scale]), try setting this to [code]true[/code].

members.name: HeightmapFlipTangent
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], flips the mesh's tangent vectors when interpreting the height map. If the heightmap effect looks strange when the camera moves (even with a reasonable [member heightmap_scale]), try setting this to [code]true[/code].

members.name: HeightmapFlipTexture
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], interprets the height map texture as a depth map, with brighter values appearing to be "lower" in altitude compared to darker values. This can be enabled for compatibility with some materials authored for Godot 3.x. This is not necessary if the Invert import option was used to invert the depth map in Godot 3.x, in which case [member heightmap_flip_texture] should remain [code]false[/code].

members.name: HeightmapMaxLayers
members.summary: The number of layers to use for parallax occlusion mapping when the camera is up close to the material. Higher values result in a more convincing depth effect, especially in materials that have steep height changes. Higher values have a significant cost on the GPU, so it should only be increased on materials where it makes a significant visual difference. [b]Note:[/b] Only effective if [member heightmap_deep_parallax] is [code]true[/code].

members.name: HeightmapMinLayers
members.summary: The number of layers to use for parallax occlusion mapping when the camera is far away from the material. Higher values result in a more convincing depth effect, especially in materials that have steep height changes. Higher values have a significant cost on the GPU, so it should only be increased on materials where it makes a significant visual difference. [b]Note:[/b] Only effective if [member heightmap_deep_parallax] is [code]true[/code].

members.name: HeightmapScale
members.summary: The heightmap scale to use for the parallax effect (see [member heightmap_enabled]). The default value is tuned so that the highest point (value = 255) appears to be 5 cm higher than the lowest point (value = 0). Higher values result in a deeper appearance, but may result in artifacts appearing when looking at the material from oblique angles, especially when the camera moves. Negative values can be used to invert the parallax effect, but this is different from inverting the texture using [member heightmap_flip_texture] as the material will also appear to be "closer" to the camera. In most cases, [member heightmap_scale] should be kept to a positive value. [b]Note:[/b] If the height map effect looks strange regardless of this value, try adjusting [member heightmap_flip_binormal] and [member heightmap_flip_tangent]. See also [member heightmap_texture] for recommendations on authoring heightmap textures, as the way the heightmap texture is authored affects how [member heightmap_scale] behaves.

members.name: HeightmapTexture
members.summary: The texture to use as a height map. See also [member heightmap_enabled]. For best results, the texture should be normalized (with [member heightmap_scale] reduced to compensate). In [url=https://gimp.org]GIMP[/url], this can be done using [b]Colors > Auto > Equalize[/b]. If the texture only uses a small part of its available range, the parallax effect may look strange, especially when the camera moves. [b]Note:[/b] To reduce memory usage and improve loading times, you may be able to use a lower-resolution heightmap texture as most heightmaps are only comprised of low-frequency data.

members.name: Metallic
members.summary: A high value makes the material appear more like a metal. Non-metals use their albedo as the diffuse color and add diffuse to the specular reflection. With non-metals, the reflection appears on top of the albedo color. Metals use their albedo as a multiplier to the specular reflection and set the diffuse color to black resulting in a tinted reflection. Materials work better when fully metal or fully non-metal, values between [code]0[/code] and [code]1[/code] should only be used for blending between metal and non-metal sections. To alter the amount of reflection use [member roughness].

members.name: MetallicSpecular
members.summary: Adjusts the strength of specular reflections. Specular reflections are composed of scene reflections and the specular lobe which is the bright spot that is reflected from light sources. When set to [code]0.0[/code], no specular reflections will be visible. This differs from the [constant SPECULAR_DISABLED] [enum SpecularMode] as [constant SPECULAR_DISABLED] only applies to the specular lobe from the light source. [b]Note:[/b] Unlike [member metallic], this is not energy-conserving, so it should be left at [code]0.5[/code] in most cases. See also [member roughness].

members.name: MetallicTexture
members.summary: Texture used to specify metallic for an object. This is multiplied by [member metallic].

members.name: MetallicTextureChannel
members.summary: Specifies the channel of the [member metallic_texture] in which the metallic information is stored. This is useful when you store the information for multiple effects in a single texture. For example if you stored metallic in the red channel, roughness in the blue, and ambient occlusion in the green you could reduce the number of textures you use.

members.name: MsdfOutlineSize
members.summary: The width of the shape outline.

members.name: MsdfPixelRange
members.summary: The width of the range around the shape between the minimum and maximum representable signed distance.

members.name: NoDepthTest
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], depth testing is disabled and the object will be drawn in render order.

members.name: NormalEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], normal mapping is enabled. This has a slight performance cost, especially on mobile GPUs.

members.name: NormalScale
members.summary: The strength of the normal map's effect.

members.name: NormalTexture
members.summary: Texture used to specify the normal at a given pixel. The [member normal_texture] only uses the red and green channels; the blue and alpha channels are ignored. The normal read from [member normal_texture] is oriented around the surface normal provided by the [Mesh]. [b]Note:[/b] The mesh must have both normals and tangents defined in its vertex data. Otherwise, the normal map won't render correctly and will only appear to darken the whole surface. If creating geometry with [SurfaceTool], you can use [method SurfaceTool.generate_normals] and [method SurfaceTool.generate_tangents] to automatically generate normals and tangents respectively. [b]Note:[/b] Godot expects the normal map to use X+, Y+, and Z+ coordinates. See [url=http://wiki.polycount.com/wiki/Normal_Map_Technical_Details#Common_Swizzle_Coordinates]this page[/url] for a comparison of normal map coordinates expected by popular engines. [b]Note:[/b] If [member detail_enabled] is [code]true[/code], the [member detail_albedo] texture is drawn [i]below[/i] the [member normal_texture]. To display a normal map [i]above[/i] the [member detail_albedo] texture, use [member detail_normal] instead.

members.name: OrmTexture
members.summary: The Occlusion/Roughness/Metallic texture to use. This is a more efficient replacement of [member ao_texture], [member roughness_texture] and [member metallic_texture] in [ORMMaterial3D]. Ambient occlusion is stored in the red channel. Roughness map is stored in the green channel. Metallic map is stored in the blue channel. The alpha channel is ignored.

members.name: ParticlesAnimHFrames
members.summary: The number of horizontal frames in the particle sprite sheet. Only enabled when using [constant BILLBOARD_PARTICLES]. See [member billboard_mode].

members.name: ParticlesAnimLoop
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], particle animations are looped. Only enabled when using [constant BILLBOARD_PARTICLES]. See [member billboard_mode].

members.name: ParticlesAnimVFrames
members.summary: The number of vertical frames in the particle sprite sheet. Only enabled when using [constant BILLBOARD_PARTICLES]. See [member billboard_mode].

members.name: PointSize
members.summary: The point size in pixels. See [member use_point_size].

members.name: ProximityFadeDistance
members.summary: Distance over which the fade effect takes place. The larger the distance the longer it takes for an object to fade.

members.name: ProximityFadeEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the proximity fade effect is enabled. The proximity fade effect fades out each pixel based on its distance to another object.

members.name: RefractionEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the refraction effect is enabled. Distorts transparency based on light from behind the object. [b]Note:[/b] Refraction is implemented using the screen texture. Only opaque materials will appear in the refraction, since transparent materials do not appear in the screen texture.

members.name: RefractionScale
members.summary: The strength of the refraction effect.

members.name: RefractionTexture
members.summary: Texture that controls the strength of the refraction per-pixel. Multiplied by [member refraction_scale].

members.name: RefractionTextureChannel
members.summary: Specifies the channel of the [member refraction_texture] in which the refraction information is stored. This is useful when you store the information for multiple effects in a single texture. For example if you stored refraction in the red channel, roughness in the blue, and ambient occlusion in the green you could reduce the number of textures you use.

members.name: Rim
members.summary: Sets the strength of the rim lighting effect.

members.name: RimEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], rim effect is enabled. Rim lighting increases the brightness at glancing angles on an object. [b]Note:[/b] Rim lighting is not visible if the material's [member shading_mode] is [constant SHADING_MODE_UNSHADED].

members.name: RimTexture
members.summary: Texture used to set the strength of the rim lighting effect per-pixel. Multiplied by [member rim].

members.name: RimTint
members.summary: The amount of to blend light and albedo color when rendering rim effect. If [code]0[/code] the light color is used, while [code]1[/code] means albedo color is used. An intermediate value generally works best.

members.name: Roughness
members.summary: Surface reflection. A value of [code]0[/code] represents a perfect mirror while a value of [code]1[/code] completely blurs the reflection. See also [member metallic].

members.name: RoughnessTexture
members.summary: Texture used to control the roughness per-pixel. Multiplied by [member roughness].

members.name: RoughnessTextureChannel
members.summary: Specifies the channel of the [member roughness_texture] in which the roughness information is stored. This is useful when you store the information for multiple effects in a single texture. For example if you stored metallic in the red channel, roughness in the blue, and ambient occlusion in the green you could reduce the number of textures you use.

members.name: ShadingMode
members.summary: Sets whether the shading takes place, per-pixel, per-vertex or unshaded. Per-vertex lighting is faster, making it the best choice for mobile applications, however it looks considerably worse than per-pixel. Unshaded rendering is the fastest, but disables all interactions with lights.

members.name: ShadowToOpacity
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], enables the "shadow to opacity" render mode where lighting modifies the alpha so shadowed areas are opaque and non-shadowed areas are transparent. Useful for overlaying shadows onto a camera feed in AR.

members.name: SpecularMode
members.summary: The method for rendering the specular blob. [b]Note:[/b] [member specular_mode] only applies to the specular blob. It does not affect specular reflections from the sky, screen-space reflections, [VoxelGI], SDFGI or [ReflectionProbe]s. To disable reflections from these sources as well, set [member metallic_specular] to [code]0.0[/code] instead.

members.name: StencilColor
members.summary: The primary color of the stencil effect.

members.name: StencilCompare
members.summary: The comparison operator to use for stencil masking operations.

members.name: StencilFlags
members.summary: The flags dictating how the stencil operation behaves.

members.name: StencilMode
members.summary: The stencil effect mode.

members.name: StencilOutlineThickness
members.summary: The outline thickness for [constant STENCIL_MODE_OUTLINE].

members.name: StencilReference
members.summary: The stencil reference value (0-255). Typically a power of 2.

members.name: SubsurfScatterEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], subsurface scattering is enabled. Emulates light that penetrates an object's surface, is scattered, and then emerges. Subsurface scattering quality is controlled by [member ProjectSettings.rendering/environment/subsurface_scattering/subsurface_scattering_quality]. [b]Note:[/b] Subsurface scattering is not supported on viewports that have a transparent background (where [member Viewport.transparent_bg] is [code]true[/code]).

members.name: SubsurfScatterSkinMode
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], subsurface scattering will use a special mode optimized for the color and density of human skin, such as boosting the intensity of the red channel in subsurface scattering.

members.name: SubsurfScatterStrength
members.summary: The strength of the subsurface scattering effect. The depth of the effect is also controlled by [member ProjectSettings.rendering/environment/subsurface_scattering/subsurface_scattering_scale], which is set globally.

members.name: SubsurfScatterTexture
members.summary: Texture used to control the subsurface scattering strength. Stored in the red texture channel. Multiplied by [member subsurf_scatter_strength].

members.name: SubsurfScatterTransmittanceBoost
members.summary: The intensity of the subsurface scattering transmittance effect.

members.name: SubsurfScatterTransmittanceColor
members.summary: The color to multiply the subsurface scattering transmittance effect with. Ignored if [member subsurf_scatter_skin_mode] is [code]true[/code].

members.name: SubsurfScatterTransmittanceDepth
members.summary: The depth of the subsurface scattering transmittance effect.

members.name: SubsurfScatterTransmittanceEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], enables subsurface scattering transmittance. Only effective if [member subsurf_scatter_enabled] is [code]true[/code]. See also [member backlight_enabled].

members.name: SubsurfScatterTransmittanceTexture
members.summary: The texture to use for multiplying the intensity of the subsurface scattering transmittance intensity. See also [member subsurf_scatter_texture]. Ignored if [member subsurf_scatter_skin_mode] is [code]true[/code].

members.name: TextureFilter
members.summary: Filter flags for the texture. [b]Note:[/b] [member heightmap_texture] is always sampled with linear filtering, even if nearest-neighbor filtering is selected here. This is to ensure the heightmap effect looks as intended. If you need sharper height transitions between pixels, resize the heightmap texture in an image editor with nearest-neighbor filtering.

members.name: TextureRepeat
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the texture repeats when exceeding the texture's size. See [constant FLAG_USE_TEXTURE_REPEAT].

members.name: Transparency
members.summary: The material's transparency mode. Some transparency modes will disable shadow casting. Any transparency mode other than [constant TRANSPARENCY_DISABLED] has a greater performance impact compared to opaque rendering. See also [member blend_mode].

members.name: UseFovOverride
members.summary: If [code]true[/code] use [member fov_override] to override the [Camera3D]'s field of view angle.

members.name: UseParticleTrails
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], enables parts of the shader required for [GPUParticles3D] trails to function. This also requires using a mesh with appropriate skinning, such as [RibbonTrailMesh] or [TubeTrailMesh]. Enabling this feature outside of materials used in [GPUParticles3D] meshes will break material rendering.

members.name: UsePointSize
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], render point size can be changed. [b]Note:[/b] This is only effective for objects whose geometry is point-based rather than triangle-based. See also [member point_size].

members.name: UseZClipScale
members.summary: If [code]true[/code] use [member z_clip_scale] to scale the object being rendered towards the camera to avoid clipping into things like walls.

members.name: Uv1Offset
members.summary: How much to offset the [code]UV[/code] coordinates. This amount will be added to [code]UV[/code] in the vertex function. This can be used to offset a texture. The Z component is used when [member uv1_triplanar] is enabled, but it is not used anywhere else.

members.name: Uv1Scale
members.summary: How much to scale the [code]UV[/code] coordinates. This is multiplied by [code]UV[/code] in the vertex function. The Z component is used when [member uv1_triplanar] is enabled, but it is not used anywhere else.

members.name: Uv1Triplanar
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], instead of using [code]UV[/code] textures will use a triplanar texture lookup to determine how to apply textures. Triplanar uses the orientation of the object's surface to blend between texture coordinates. It reads from the source texture 3 times, once for each axis and then blends between the results based on how closely the pixel aligns with each axis. This is often used for natural features to get a realistic blend of materials. Because triplanar texturing requires many more texture reads per-pixel it is much slower than normal UV texturing. Additionally, because it is blending the texture between the three axes, it is unsuitable when you are trying to achieve crisp texturing.

members.name: Uv1TriplanarSharpness
members.summary: A lower number blends the texture more softly while a higher number blends the texture more sharply. [b]Note:[/b] [member uv1_triplanar_sharpness] is clamped between [code]0.0[/code] and [code]150.0[/code] (inclusive) as values outside that range can look broken depending on the mesh.

members.name: Uv1WorldTriplanar
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], triplanar mapping for [code]UV[/code] is calculated in world space rather than object local space. See also [member uv1_triplanar].

members.name: Uv2Offset
members.summary: How much to offset the [code]UV2[/code] coordinates. This amount will be added to [code]UV2[/code] in the vertex function. This can be used to offset a texture. The Z component is used when [member uv2_triplanar] is enabled, but it is not used anywhere else.

members.name: Uv2Scale
members.summary: How much to scale the [code]UV2[/code] coordinates. This is multiplied by [code]UV2[/code] in the vertex function. The Z component is used when [member uv2_triplanar] is enabled, but it is not used anywhere else.

members.name: Uv2Triplanar
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], instead of using [code]UV2[/code] textures will use a triplanar texture lookup to determine how to apply textures. Triplanar uses the orientation of the object's surface to blend between texture coordinates. It reads from the source texture 3 times, once for each axis and then blends between the results based on how closely the pixel aligns with each axis. This is often used for natural features to get a realistic blend of materials. Because triplanar texturing requires many more texture reads per-pixel it is much slower than normal UV texturing. Additionally, because it is blending the texture between the three axes, it is unsuitable when you are trying to achieve crisp texturing.

members.name: Uv2TriplanarSharpness
members.summary: A lower number blends the texture more softly while a higher number blends the texture more sharply. [b]Note:[/b] [member uv2_triplanar_sharpness] is clamped between [code]0.0[/code] and [code]150.0[/code] (inclusive) as values outside that range can look broken depending on the mesh.

members.name: Uv2WorldTriplanar
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], triplanar mapping for [code]UV2[/code] is calculated in world space rather than object local space. See also [member uv2_triplanar].

members.name: VertexColorIsSrgb
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], vertex colors are considered to be stored in nonlinear sRGB encoding and are converted to linear encoding during rendering. If [code]false[/code], vertex colors are considered to be stored in linear encoding and are rendered as-is. See also [member albedo_texture_force_srgb]. [b]Note:[/b] Only effective when using the Forward+ and Mobile rendering methods, not Compatibility.

members.name: VertexColorUseAsAlbedo
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the vertex color is used as albedo color.

members.name: ZClipScale
members.summary: Scales the object being rendered towards the camera to avoid clipping into things like walls. This is intended to be used for objects that are fixed with respect to the camera like player arms, tools, etc. Lighting and shadows will continue to work correctly when this setting is adjusted, but screen-space effects like SSAO and SSR may break with lower scales. Therefore, try to keep this setting as close to [code]1.0[/code] as possible.

values.name: ALPHA_ANTIALIASING_ALPHA_TO_COVERAGE
values.summary: Enables AlphaToCoverage. Alpha values in the material are passed to the AntiAliasing sample mask.

values.name: ALPHA_ANTIALIASING_ALPHA_TO_COVERAGE_AND_TO_ONE
values.summary: Enables AlphaToCoverage and forces all non-zero alpha values to [code]1[/code]. Alpha values in the material are passed to the AntiAliasing sample mask.

values.name: ALPHA_ANTIALIASING_OFF
values.summary: Disables Alpha AntiAliasing for the material.

values.name: BILLBOARD_DISABLED
values.summary: Billboard mode is disabled.

values.name: BILLBOARD_ENABLED
values.summary: The object's Z axis will always face the camera.

values.name: BILLBOARD_FIXED_Y
values.summary: The object's X axis will always face the camera.

values.name: BILLBOARD_PARTICLES
values.summary: Used for particle systems when assigned to [GPUParticles3D] and [CPUParticles3D] nodes (flipbook animation). Enables [code]particles_anim_*[/code] properties. The [member ParticleProcessMaterial.anim_speed_min] or [member CPUParticles3D.anim_speed_min] should also be set to a value bigger than zero for the animation to play.

values.name: BLEND_MODE_ADD
values.summary: The color of the object is added to the background.

values.name: BLEND_MODE_MIX
values.summary: Default blend mode. The color of the object is blended over the background based on the object's alpha value.

values.name: BLEND_MODE_MUL
values.summary: The color of the object is multiplied by the background.

values.name: BLEND_MODE_PREMULT_ALPHA
values.summary: The color of the object is added to the background and the alpha channel is used to mask out the background. This is effectively a hybrid of the blend mix and add modes, useful for effects like fire where you want the flame to add but the smoke to mix. By default, this works with unshaded materials using premultiplied textures. For shaded materials, use the [code]PREMUL_ALPHA_FACTOR[/code] built-in so that lighting can be modulated as well.

values.name: BLEND_MODE_SUB
values.summary: The color of the object is subtracted from the background.

values.name: CULL_BACK
values.summary: Default cull mode. The back of the object is culled when not visible. Back face triangles will be culled when facing the camera. This results in only the front side of triangles being drawn. For closed-surface meshes, this means that only the exterior of the mesh will be visible.

values.name: CULL_DISABLED
values.summary: No face culling is performed; both the front face and back face will be visible.

values.name: CULL_FRONT
values.summary: Front face triangles will be culled when facing the camera. This results in only the back side of triangles being drawn. For closed-surface meshes, this means that the interior of the mesh will be drawn instead of the exterior.

values.name: DEPTH_DRAW_ALWAYS
values.summary: Objects will write to depth during the opaque and the transparent passes. Transparent objects that are close to the camera may obscure other transparent objects behind them. [b]Note:[/b] This does not influence whether transparent objects are included in the depth prepass or not. For that, see [enum Transparency].

values.name: DEPTH_DRAW_DISABLED
values.summary: Objects will not write their depth to the depth buffer, even during the depth prepass (if enabled).

values.name: DEPTH_DRAW_OPAQUE_ONLY
values.summary: Default depth draw mode. Depth is drawn only for opaque objects during the opaque prepass (if any) and during the opaque pass.

values.name: DEPTH_TEST_DEFAULT
values.summary: Depth test will discard the pixel if it is behind other pixels.

values.name: DEPTH_TEST_INVERTED
values.summary: Depth test will discard the pixel if it is in front of other pixels. Useful for stencil effects.

values.name: DETAIL_UV_1
values.summary: Use [code]UV[/code] with the detail texture.

values.name: DETAIL_UV_2
values.summary: Use [code]UV2[/code] with the detail texture.

values.name: DIFFUSE_BURLEY
values.summary: Default diffuse scattering algorithm.

values.name: DIFFUSE_LAMBERT
values.summary: Diffuse scattering ignores roughness.

values.name: DIFFUSE_LAMBERT_WRAP
values.summary: Extends Lambert to cover more than 90 degrees when roughness increases.

values.name: DIFFUSE_TOON
values.summary: Uses a hard cut for lighting, with smoothing affected by roughness.

values.name: DISTANCE_FADE_DISABLED
values.summary: Do not use distance fade.

values.name: DISTANCE_FADE_OBJECT_DITHER
values.summary: Smoothly fades the object out based on the object's distance from the camera using a dithering approach. Dithering discards pixels based on a set pattern to smoothly fade without enabling transparency. On certain hardware, this can be faster than [constant DISTANCE_FADE_PIXEL_ALPHA] and [constant DISTANCE_FADE_PIXEL_DITHER].

values.name: DISTANCE_FADE_PIXEL_ALPHA
values.summary: Smoothly fades the object out based on each pixel's distance from the camera using the alpha channel.

values.name: DISTANCE_FADE_PIXEL_DITHER
values.summary: Smoothly fades the object out based on each pixel's distance from the camera using a dithering approach. Dithering discards pixels based on a set pattern to smoothly fade without enabling transparency. On certain hardware, this can be faster than [constant DISTANCE_FADE_PIXEL_ALPHA].

values.name: EMISSION_OP_ADD
values.summary: Adds the emission color to the color from the emission texture.

values.name: EMISSION_OP_MULTIPLY
values.summary: Multiplies the emission color by the color from the emission texture.

values.name: FEATURE_AMBIENT_OCCLUSION
values.summary: Constant for setting [member ao_enabled].

values.name: FEATURE_ANISOTROPY
values.summary: Constant for setting [member anisotropy_enabled].

values.name: FEATURE_BACKLIGHT
values.summary: Constant for setting [member backlight_enabled].

values.name: FEATURE_BENT_NORMAL_MAPPING
values.summary: Constant for setting [member bent_normal_enabled].

values.name: FEATURE_CLEARCOAT
values.summary: Constant for setting [member clearcoat_enabled].

values.name: FEATURE_DETAIL
values.summary: Constant for setting [member detail_enabled].

values.name: FEATURE_EMISSION
values.summary: Constant for setting [member emission_enabled].

values.name: FEATURE_HEIGHT_MAPPING
values.summary: Constant for setting [member heightmap_enabled].

values.name: FEATURE_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum Feature] enum.

values.name: FEATURE_NORMAL_MAPPING
values.summary: Constant for setting [member normal_enabled].

values.name: FEATURE_REFRACTION
values.summary: Constant for setting [member refraction_enabled].

values.name: FEATURE_RIM
values.summary: Constant for setting [member rim_enabled].

values.name: FEATURE_SUBSURFACE_SCATTERING
values.summary: Constant for setting [member subsurf_scatter_enabled].

values.name: FEATURE_SUBSURFACE_TRANSMITTANCE
values.summary: Constant for setting [member subsurf_scatter_transmittance_enabled].

values.name: FLAG_ALBEDO_FROM_VERTEX_COLOR
values.summary: Set [code]ALBEDO[/code] to the per-vertex color specified in the mesh.

values.name: FLAG_ALBEDO_TEXTURE_FORCE_SRGB
values.summary: Forces the shader to convert albedo from nonlinear sRGB encoding to linear encoding. See also [member albedo_texture_force_srgb].

values.name: FLAG_ALBEDO_TEXTURE_MSDF
values.summary: Enables multichannel signed distance field rendering shader.

values.name: FLAG_AO_ON_UV2
values.summary: Use [code]UV2[/code] coordinates to look up from the [member ao_texture].

values.name: FLAG_BILLBOARD_KEEP_SCALE
values.summary: Shader will keep the scale set for the mesh. Otherwise the scale is lost when billboarding. Only applies when [member billboard_mode] is [constant BILLBOARD_ENABLED].

values.name: FLAG_DISABLE_AMBIENT_LIGHT
values.summary: Disables receiving ambient light.

values.name: FLAG_DISABLE_DEPTH_TEST
values.summary: Disables the depth test, so this object is drawn on top of all others drawn before it. This puts the object in the transparent draw pass where it is sorted based on distance to camera. Objects drawn after it in the draw order may cover it. This also disables writing to depth.

values.name: FLAG_DISABLE_FOG
values.summary: Disables receiving depth-based or volumetric fog.

values.name: FLAG_DISABLE_SPECULAR_OCCLUSION
values.summary: Disables specular occlusion.

values.name: FLAG_DONT_RECEIVE_SHADOWS
values.summary: Disables receiving shadows from other objects.

values.name: FLAG_EMISSION_ON_UV2
values.summary: Use [code]UV2[/code] coordinates to look up from the [member emission_texture].

values.name: FLAG_FIXED_SIZE
values.summary: Object is scaled by depth so that it always appears the same size on screen.

values.name: FLAG_INVERT_HEIGHTMAP
values.summary: Invert values read from a depth texture to convert them to height values (heightmap).

values.name: FLAG_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum Flags] enum.

values.name: FLAG_PARTICLE_TRAILS_MODE
values.summary: Enables parts of the shader required for [GPUParticles3D] trails to function. This also requires using a mesh with appropriate skinning, such as [RibbonTrailMesh] or [TubeTrailMesh]. Enabling this feature outside of materials used in [GPUParticles3D] meshes will break material rendering.

values.name: FLAG_SRGB_VERTEX_COLOR
values.summary: Vertex colors are considered to be stored in nonlinear sRGB encoding and are converted to linear encoding during rendering. See also [member vertex_color_is_srgb]. [b]Note:[/b] Only effective when using the Forward+ and Mobile rendering methods.

values.name: FLAG_SUBSURFACE_MODE_SKIN
values.summary: Enables the skin mode for subsurface scattering which is used to improve the look of subsurface scattering when used for human skin.

values.name: FLAG_USE_FOV_OVERRIDE
values.summary: Enables using [member fov_override].

values.name: FLAG_USE_POINT_SIZE
values.summary: Uses point size to alter the size of primitive points. Also changes the albedo texture lookup to use [code]POINT_COORD[/code] instead of [code]UV[/code].

values.name: FLAG_USE_SHADOW_TO_OPACITY
values.summary: Enables the shadow to opacity feature.

values.name: FLAG_USE_TEXTURE_REPEAT
values.summary: Enables the texture to repeat when UV coordinates are outside the 0-1 range. If using one of the linear filtering modes, this can result in artifacts at the edges of a texture when the sampler filters across the edges of the texture.

values.name: FLAG_USE_Z_CLIP_SCALE
values.summary: Enables using [member z_clip_scale].

values.name: FLAG_UV1_USE_TRIPLANAR
values.summary: Use triplanar texture lookup for all texture lookups that would normally use [code]UV[/code].

values.name: FLAG_UV1_USE_WORLD_TRIPLANAR
values.summary: Use triplanar texture lookup for all texture lookups that would normally use [code]UV[/code].

values.name: FLAG_UV2_USE_TRIPLANAR
values.summary: Use triplanar texture lookup for all texture lookups that would normally use [code]UV2[/code].

values.name: FLAG_UV2_USE_WORLD_TRIPLANAR
values.summary: Use triplanar texture lookup for all texture lookups that would normally use [code]UV2[/code].

values.name: SHADING_MODE_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ShadingMode] enum.

values.name: SHADING_MODE_PER_PIXEL
values.summary: The object will be shaded per pixel. Useful for realistic shading effects.

values.name: SHADING_MODE_PER_VERTEX
values.summary: The object will be shaded per vertex. Useful when you want cheaper shaders and do not care about visual quality.

values.name: SHADING_MODE_UNSHADED
values.summary: The object will not receive shadows. This is the fastest to render, but it disables all interactions with lights.

values.name: SPECULAR_DISABLED
values.summary: No specular blob. This is slightly faster to render than other specular modes.

values.name: SPECULAR_SCHLICK_GGX
values.summary: Default specular blob. [b]Note:[/b] Forward+ uses multiscattering for more accurate reflections, although the impact of multiscattering is more noticeable on rough metallic surfaces than on smooth, non-metallic surfaces. [b]Note:[/b] Mobile and Compatibility don't perform multiscattering for performance reasons. Instead, they perform single scattering, which means rough metallic surfaces may look slightly darker than intended.

values.name: SPECULAR_TOON
values.summary: Toon blob which changes size based on roughness.

values.name: STENCIL_COMPARE_ALWAYS
values.summary: Always passes the stencil test.

values.name: STENCIL_COMPARE_EQUAL
values.summary: Passes the stencil test when the reference value is equal to the existing stencil value.

values.name: STENCIL_COMPARE_GREATER
values.summary: Passes the stencil test when the reference value is greater than the existing stencil value.

values.name: STENCIL_COMPARE_GREATER_OR_EQUAL
values.summary: Passes the stencil test when the reference value is greater than or equal to the existing stencil value.

values.name: STENCIL_COMPARE_LESS
values.summary: Passes the stencil test when the reference value is less than the existing stencil value.

values.name: STENCIL_COMPARE_LESS_OR_EQUAL
values.summary: Passes the stencil test when the reference value is less than or equal to the existing stencil value.

values.name: STENCIL_COMPARE_NOT_EQUAL
values.summary: Passes the stencil test when the reference value is not equal to the existing stencil value.

values.name: STENCIL_FLAG_READ
values.summary: The material will only be rendered where it passes a stencil comparison with existing stencil buffer values.

values.name: STENCIL_FLAG_WRITE
values.summary: The material will write the reference value to the stencil buffer where it passes the depth test.

values.name: STENCIL_FLAG_WRITE_DEPTH_FAIL
values.summary: The material will write the reference value to the stencil buffer where it fails the depth test.

values.name: STENCIL_MODE_CUSTOM
values.summary: Enables stencil operations without a preset.

values.name: STENCIL_MODE_DISABLED
values.summary: Disables stencil operations.

values.name: STENCIL_MODE_OUTLINE
values.summary: Stencil preset which applies an outline to the object. [b]Note:[/b] Requires a [member Material.next_pass] material which will be automatically applied. Any manual changes made to [member Material.next_pass] will be lost when the stencil properties are modified or the scene is reloaded. To safely apply a [member Material.next_pass] material on a material that uses stencil presets, use [member GeometryInstance3D.material_overlay] instead.

values.name: STENCIL_MODE_XRAY
values.summary: Stencil preset which shows a silhouette of the object behind walls. [b]Note:[/b] Requires a [member Material.next_pass] material which will be automatically applied. Any manual changes made to [member Material.next_pass] will be lost when the stencil properties are modified or the scene is reloaded. To safely apply a [member Material.next_pass] material on a material that uses stencil presets, use [member GeometryInstance3D.material_overlay] instead.

values.name: TEXTURE_CHANNEL_ALPHA
values.summary: Used to read from the alpha channel of a texture.

values.name: TEXTURE_CHANNEL_BLUE
values.summary: Used to read from the blue channel of a texture.

values.name: TEXTURE_CHANNEL_GRAYSCALE
values.summary: Used to read from the linear (non-perceptual) average of the red, green and blue channels of a texture.

values.name: TEXTURE_CHANNEL_GREEN
values.summary: Used to read from the green channel of a texture.

values.name: TEXTURE_CHANNEL_RED
values.summary: Used to read from the red channel of a texture.

values.name: TEXTURE_FILTER_LINEAR
values.summary: The texture filter blends between the nearest 4 pixels. This makes the texture look smooth from up close, and grainy from a distance (due to mipmaps not being sampled).

values.name: TEXTURE_FILTER_LINEAR_WITH_MIPMAPS
values.summary: The texture filter blends between the nearest 4 pixels and between the nearest 2 mipmaps (or uses the nearest mipmap if [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/use_nearest_mipmap_filter] is [code]true[/code]). This makes the texture look smooth from up close, and smooth from a distance.

values.name: TEXTURE_FILTER_LINEAR_WITH_MIPMAPS_ANISOTROPIC
values.summary: The texture filter blends between the nearest 4 pixels and blends between 2 mipmaps (or uses the nearest mipmap if [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/use_nearest_mipmap_filter] is [code]true[/code]) based on the angle between the surface and the camera view. This makes the texture look smooth from up close, and smooth from a distance. Anisotropic filtering improves texture quality on surfaces that are almost in line with the camera, but is slightly slower. The anisotropic filtering level can be changed by adjusting [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/anisotropic_filtering_level].

values.name: TEXTURE_FILTER_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum TextureFilter] enum.

values.name: TEXTURE_FILTER_NEAREST
values.summary: The texture filter reads from the nearest pixel only. This makes the texture look pixelated from up close, and grainy from a distance (due to mipmaps not being sampled).

values.name: TEXTURE_FILTER_NEAREST_WITH_MIPMAPS
values.summary: The texture filter reads from the nearest pixel and blends between the nearest 2 mipmaps (or uses the nearest mipmap if [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/use_nearest_mipmap_filter] is [code]true[/code]). This makes the texture look pixelated from up close, and smooth from a distance.

values.name: TEXTURE_FILTER_NEAREST_WITH_MIPMAPS_ANISOTROPIC
values.summary: The texture filter reads from the nearest pixel and blends between 2 mipmaps (or uses the nearest mipmap if [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/use_nearest_mipmap_filter] is [code]true[/code]) based on the angle between the surface and the camera view. This makes the texture look pixelated from up close, and smooth from a distance. Anisotropic filtering improves texture quality on surfaces that are almost in line with the camera, but is slightly slower. The anisotropic filtering level can be changed by adjusting [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/anisotropic_filtering_level].

values.name: TEXTURE_ALBEDO
values.summary: Texture specifying per-pixel color.

values.name: TEXTURE_AMBIENT_OCCLUSION
values.summary: Texture specifying per-pixel ambient occlusion value.

values.name: TEXTURE_BACKLIGHT
values.summary: Texture specifying per-pixel backlight color.

values.name: TEXTURE_BENT_NORMAL
values.summary: Texture specifying per-pixel bent normal vector.

values.name: TEXTURE_CLEARCOAT
values.summary: Texture specifying per-pixel clearcoat value.

values.name: TEXTURE_DETAIL_ALBEDO
values.summary: Texture specifying per-pixel detail color.

values.name: TEXTURE_DETAIL_MASK
values.summary: Texture specifying per-pixel detail mask blending value.

values.name: TEXTURE_DETAIL_NORMAL
values.summary: Texture specifying per-pixel detail normal.

values.name: TEXTURE_EMISSION
values.summary: Texture specifying per-pixel emission color.

values.name: TEXTURE_FLOWMAP
values.summary: Texture specifying per-pixel flowmap direction for use with [member anisotropy].

values.name: TEXTURE_HEIGHTMAP
values.summary: Texture specifying per-pixel height.

values.name: TEXTURE_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum TextureParam] enum.

values.name: TEXTURE_METALLIC
values.summary: Texture specifying per-pixel metallic value.

values.name: TEXTURE_NORMAL
values.summary: Texture specifying per-pixel normal vector.

values.name: TEXTURE_ORM
values.summary: Texture holding ambient occlusion, roughness, and metallic.

values.name: TEXTURE_REFRACTION
values.summary: Texture specifying per-pixel refraction strength.

values.name: TEXTURE_RIM
values.summary: Texture specifying per-pixel rim value.

values.name: TEXTURE_ROUGHNESS
values.summary: Texture specifying per-pixel roughness value.

values.name: TEXTURE_SUBSURFACE_SCATTERING
values.summary: Texture specifying per-pixel subsurface scattering.

values.name: TEXTURE_SUBSURFACE_TRANSMITTANCE
values.summary: Texture specifying per-pixel transmittance for subsurface scattering.

values.name: TRANSPARENCY_ALPHA
values.summary: The material will use the texture's alpha values for transparency. This is the slowest to render, and disables shadow casting.

values.name: TRANSPARENCY_ALPHA_DEPTH_PRE_PASS
values.summary: The material will use the texture's alpha value for transparency, but will discard fragments with an alpha of less than 0.99 during the depth prepass and fragments with an alpha less than 0.1 during the shadow pass. This also supports casting shadows.

values.name: TRANSPARENCY_ALPHA_HASH
values.summary: The material will cut off all values below a spatially-deterministic threshold, the rest will remain opaque. This is faster to render than alpha blending, but slower than opaque rendering. This also supports casting shadows. Alpha hashing is suited for hair rendering.

values.name: TRANSPARENCY_ALPHA_SCISSOR
values.summary: The material will cut off all values below a threshold, the rest will remain opaque. The opaque portions will be rendered in the depth prepass. This is faster to render than alpha blending, but slower than opaque rendering. This also supports casting shadows.

values.name: TRANSPARENCY_DISABLED
values.summary: The material will not use transparency. This is the fastest to render.

constants.name: TEXTURE_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum TextureParam] enum.

constants.name: TEXTURE_FILTER_NEAREST
constants.summary: The texture filter reads from the nearest pixel only. This makes the texture look pixelated from up close, and grainy from a distance (due to mipmaps not being sampled).

constants.name: TEXTURE_FILTER_LINEAR
constants.summary: The texture filter blends between the nearest 4 pixels. This makes the texture look smooth from up close, and grainy from a distance (due to mipmaps not being sampled).

constants.name: TEXTURE_FILTER_NEAREST_WITH_MIPMAPS
constants.summary: The texture filter reads from the nearest pixel and blends between the nearest 2 mipmaps (or uses the nearest mipmap if [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/use_nearest_mipmap_filter] is [code]true[/code]). This makes the texture look pixelated from up close, and smooth from a distance.

constants.name: TEXTURE_FILTER_LINEAR_WITH_MIPMAPS
constants.summary: The texture filter blends between the nearest 4 pixels and between the nearest 2 mipmaps (or uses the nearest mipmap if [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/use_nearest_mipmap_filter] is [code]true[/code]). This makes the texture look smooth from up close, and smooth from a distance.

constants.name: TEXTURE_FILTER_NEAREST_WITH_MIPMAPS_ANISOTROPIC
constants.summary: The texture filter reads from the nearest pixel and blends between 2 mipmaps (or uses the nearest mipmap if [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/use_nearest_mipmap_filter] is [code]true[/code]) based on the angle between the surface and the camera view. This makes the texture look pixelated from up close, and smooth from a distance. Anisotropic filtering improves texture quality on surfaces that are almost in line with the camera, but is slightly slower. The anisotropic filtering level can be changed by adjusting [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/anisotropic_filtering_level].

constants.name: TEXTURE_FILTER_LINEAR_WITH_MIPMAPS_ANISOTROPIC
constants.summary: The texture filter blends between the nearest 4 pixels and blends between 2 mipmaps (or uses the nearest mipmap if [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/use_nearest_mipmap_filter] is [code]true[/code]) based on the angle between the surface and the camera view. This makes the texture look smooth from up close, and smooth from a distance. Anisotropic filtering improves texture quality on surfaces that are almost in line with the camera, but is slightly slower. The anisotropic filtering level can be changed by adjusting [member ProjectSettings.rendering/textures/default_filters/anisotropic_filtering_level].

constants.name: TEXTURE_FILTER_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum TextureFilter] enum.

constants.name: DETAIL_UV_1
constants.summary: Use [code]UV[/code] with the detail texture.

constants.name: DETAIL_UV_2
constants.summary: Use [code]UV2[/code] with the detail texture.

constants.name: TRANSPARENCY_DISABLED
constants.summary: The material will not use transparency. This is the fastest to render.

constants.name: TRANSPARENCY_ALPHA
constants.summary: The material will use the texture's alpha values for transparency. This is the slowest to render, and disables shadow casting.

constants.name: TRANSPARENCY_ALPHA_SCISSOR
constants.summary: The material will cut off all values below a threshold, the rest will remain opaque. The opaque portions will be rendered in the depth prepass. This is faster to render than alpha blending, but slower than opaque rendering. This also supports casting shadows.

constants.name: TRANSPARENCY_ALPHA_HASH
constants.summary: The material will cut off all values below a spatially-deterministic threshold, the rest will remain opaque. This is faster to render than alpha blending, but slower than opaque rendering. This also supports casting shadows. Alpha hashing is suited for hair rendering.

constants.name: TRANSPARENCY_ALPHA_DEPTH_PRE_PASS
constants.summary: The material will use the texture's alpha value for transparency, but will discard fragments with an alpha of less than 0.99 during the depth prepass and fragments with an alpha less than 0.1 during the shadow pass. This also supports casting shadows.

constants.name: TRANSPARENCY_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum Transparency] enum.

constants.name: SHADING_MODE_UNSHADED
constants.summary: The object will not receive shadows. This is the fastest to render, but it disables all interactions with lights.

constants.name: SHADING_MODE_PER_PIXEL
constants.summary: The object will be shaded per pixel. Useful for realistic shading effects.

constants.name: SHADING_MODE_PER_VERTEX
constants.summary: The object will be shaded per vertex. Useful when you want cheaper shaders and do not care about visual quality.

constants.name: SHADING_MODE_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum ShadingMode] enum.

constants.name: BLEND_MODE_MIX
constants.summary: Default blend mode. The color of the object is blended over the background based on the object's alpha value.

constants.name: BLEND_MODE_ADD
constants.summary: The color of the object is added to the background.

constants.name: BLEND_MODE_SUB
constants.summary: The color of the object is subtracted from the background.

constants.name: BLEND_MODE_MUL
constants.summary: The color of the object is multiplied by the background.

constants.name: BLEND_MODE_PREMULT_ALPHA
constants.summary: The color of the object is added to the background and the alpha channel is used to mask out the background. This is effectively a hybrid of the blend mix and add modes, useful for effects like fire where you want the flame to add but the smoke to mix. By default, this works with unshaded materials using premultiplied textures. For shaded materials, use the [code]PREMUL_ALPHA_FACTOR[/code] built-in so that lighting can be modulated as well.

constants.name: ALPHA_ANTIALIASING_OFF
constants.summary: Disables Alpha AntiAliasing for the material.

constants.name: ALPHA_ANTIALIASING_ALPHA_TO_COVERAGE
constants.summary: Enables AlphaToCoverage. Alpha values in the material are passed to the AntiAliasing sample mask.

constants.name: ALPHA_ANTIALIASING_ALPHA_TO_COVERAGE_AND_TO_ONE
constants.summary: Enables AlphaToCoverage and forces all non-zero alpha values to [code]1[/code]. Alpha values in the material are passed to the AntiAliasing sample mask.

constants.name: DEPTH_DRAW_OPAQUE_ONLY
constants.summary: Default depth draw mode. Depth is drawn only for opaque objects during the opaque prepass (if any) and during the opaque pass.

constants.name: DEPTH_DRAW_ALWAYS
constants.summary: Objects will write to depth during the opaque and the transparent passes. Transparent objects that are close to the camera may obscure other transparent objects behind them. [b]Note:[/b] This does not influence whether transparent objects are included in the depth prepass or not. For that, see [enum Transparency].

constants.name: DEPTH_DRAW_DISABLED
constants.summary: Objects will not write their depth to the depth buffer, even during the depth prepass (if enabled).

constants.name: DEPTH_TEST_DEFAULT
constants.summary: Depth test will discard the pixel if it is behind other pixels.

constants.name: DEPTH_TEST_INVERTED
constants.summary: Depth test will discard the pixel if it is in front of other pixels. Useful for stencil effects.

constants.name: CULL_BACK
constants.summary: Default cull mode. The back of the object is culled when not visible. Back face triangles will be culled when facing the camera. This results in only the front side of triangles being drawn. For closed-surface meshes, this means that only the exterior of the mesh will be visible.

constants.name: CULL_FRONT
constants.summary: Front face triangles will be culled when facing the camera. This results in only the back side of triangles being drawn. For closed-surface meshes, this means that the interior of the mesh will be drawn instead of the exterior.

constants.name: CULL_DISABLED
constants.summary: No face culling is performed; both the front face and back face will be visible.

constants.name: FLAG_DISABLE_DEPTH_TEST
constants.summary: Disables the depth test, so this object is drawn on top of all others drawn before it. This puts the object in the transparent draw pass where it is sorted based on distance to camera. Objects drawn after it in the draw order may cover it. This also disables writing to depth.

constants.name: FLAG_ALBEDO_FROM_VERTEX_COLOR
constants.summary: Set [code]ALBEDO[/code] to the per-vertex color specified in the mesh.

constants.name: FLAG_SRGB_VERTEX_COLOR
constants.summary: Vertex colors are considered to be stored in nonlinear sRGB encoding and are converted to linear encoding during rendering. See also [member vertex_color_is_srgb]. [b]Note:[/b] Only effective when using the Forward+ and Mobile rendering methods.

constants.name: FLAG_USE_POINT_SIZE
constants.summary: Uses point size to alter the size of primitive points. Also changes the albedo texture lookup to use [code]POINT_COORD[/code] instead of [code]UV[/code].

constants.name: FLAG_FIXED_SIZE
constants.summary: Object is scaled by depth so that it always appears the same size on screen.

constants.name: FLAG_BILLBOARD_KEEP_SCALE
constants.summary: Shader will keep the scale set for the mesh. Otherwise the scale is lost when billboarding. Only applies when [member billboard_mode] is [constant BILLBOARD_ENABLED].

constants.name: FLAG_UV1_USE_TRIPLANAR
constants.summary: Use triplanar texture lookup for all texture lookups that would normally use [code]UV[/code].

constants.name: FLAG_UV2_USE_TRIPLANAR
constants.summary: Use triplanar texture lookup for all texture lookups that would normally use [code]UV2[/code].

constants.name: FLAG_UV1_USE_WORLD_TRIPLANAR
constants.summary: Use triplanar texture lookup for all texture lookups that would normally use [code]UV[/code].

constants.name: FLAG_UV2_USE_WORLD_TRIPLANAR
constants.summary: Use triplanar texture lookup for all texture lookups that would normally use [code]UV2[/code].

constants.name: FLAG_AO_ON_UV2
constants.summary: Use [code]UV2[/code] coordinates to look up from the [member ao_texture].

constants.name: FLAG_EMISSION_ON_UV2
constants.summary: Use [code]UV2[/code] coordinates to look up from the [member emission_texture].

constants.name: FLAG_ALBEDO_TEXTURE_FORCE_SRGB
constants.summary: Forces the shader to convert albedo from nonlinear sRGB encoding to linear encoding. See also [member albedo_texture_force_srgb].

constants.name: FLAG_DONT_RECEIVE_SHADOWS
constants.summary: Disables receiving shadows from other objects.

constants.name: FLAG_DISABLE_AMBIENT_LIGHT
constants.summary: Disables receiving ambient light.

constants.name: FLAG_USE_SHADOW_TO_OPACITY
constants.summary: Enables the shadow to opacity feature.

constants.name: FLAG_USE_TEXTURE_REPEAT
constants.summary: Enables the texture to repeat when UV coordinates are outside the 0-1 range. If using one of the linear filtering modes, this can result in artifacts at the edges of a texture when the sampler filters across the edges of the texture.

constants.name: FLAG_INVERT_HEIGHTMAP
constants.summary: Invert values read from a depth texture to convert them to height values (heightmap).

constants.name: FLAG_SUBSURFACE_MODE_SKIN
constants.summary: Enables the skin mode for subsurface scattering which is used to improve the look of subsurface scattering when used for human skin.

constants.name: FLAG_PARTICLE_TRAILS_MODE
constants.summary: Enables parts of the shader required for [GPUParticles3D] trails to function. This also requires using a mesh with appropriate skinning, such as [RibbonTrailMesh] or [TubeTrailMesh]. Enabling this feature outside of materials used in [GPUParticles3D] meshes will break material rendering.

constants.name: FLAG_ALBEDO_TEXTURE_MSDF
constants.summary: Enables multichannel signed distance field rendering shader.

constants.name: FLAG_DISABLE_FOG
constants.summary: Disables receiving depth-based or volumetric fog.

constants.name: FLAG_DISABLE_SPECULAR_OCCLUSION
constants.summary: Disables specular occlusion.

constants.name: FLAG_USE_Z_CLIP_SCALE
constants.summary: Enables using [member z_clip_scale].

constants.name: FLAG_USE_FOV_OVERRIDE
constants.summary: Enables using [member fov_override].

constants.name: FLAG_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum Flags] enum.

constants.name: DIFFUSE_BURLEY
constants.summary: Default diffuse scattering algorithm.

constants.name: DIFFUSE_LAMBERT
constants.summary: Diffuse scattering ignores roughness.

constants.name: DIFFUSE_LAMBERT_WRAP
constants.summary: Extends Lambert to cover more than 90 degrees when roughness increases.

constants.name: DIFFUSE_TOON
constants.summary: Uses a hard cut for lighting, with smoothing affected by roughness.

constants.name: SPECULAR_SCHLICK_GGX
constants.summary: Default specular blob. [b]Note:[/b] Forward+ uses multiscattering for more accurate reflections, although the impact of multiscattering is more noticeable on rough metallic surfaces than on smooth, non-metallic surfaces. [b]Note:[/b] Mobile and Compatibility don't perform multiscattering for performance reasons. Instead, they perform single scattering, which means rough metallic surfaces may look slightly darker than intended.

constants.name: SPECULAR_TOON
constants.summary: Toon blob which changes size based on roughness.

constants.name: SPECULAR_DISABLED
constants.summary: No specular blob. This is slightly faster to render than other specular modes.

constants.name: BILLBOARD_DISABLED
constants.summary: Billboard mode is disabled.

constants.name: BILLBOARD_ENABLED
constants.summary: The object's Z axis will always face the camera.

constants.name: BILLBOARD_FIXED_Y
constants.summary: The object's X axis will always face the camera.

constants.name: BILLBOARD_PARTICLES
constants.summary: Used for particle systems when assigned to [GPUParticles3D] and [CPUParticles3D] nodes (flipbook animation). Enables [code]particles_anim_*[/code] properties. The [member ParticleProcessMaterial.anim_speed_min] or [member CPUParticles3D.anim_speed_min] should also be set to a value bigger than zero for the animation to play.

constants.name: TEXTURE_CHANNEL_RED
constants.summary: Used to read from the red channel of a texture.

constants.name: TEXTURE_CHANNEL_GREEN
constants.summary: Used to read from the green channel of a texture.

constants.name: TEXTURE_CHANNEL_BLUE
constants.summary: Used to read from the blue channel of a texture.

constants.name: TEXTURE_CHANNEL_ALPHA
constants.summary: Used to read from the alpha channel of a texture.

constants.name: TEXTURE_CHANNEL_GRAYSCALE
constants.summary: Used to read from the linear (non-perceptual) average of the red, green and blue channels of a texture.

constants.name: EMISSION_OP_ADD
constants.summary: Adds the emission color to the color from the emission texture.

constants.name: EMISSION_OP_MULTIPLY
constants.summary: Multiplies the emission color by the color from the emission texture.

constants.name: DISTANCE_FADE_DISABLED
constants.summary: Do not use distance fade.

constants.name: DISTANCE_FADE_PIXEL_ALPHA
constants.summary: Smoothly fades the object out based on each pixel's distance from the camera using the alpha channel.

constants.name: DISTANCE_FADE_PIXEL_DITHER
constants.summary: Smoothly fades the object out based on each pixel's distance from the camera using a dithering approach. Dithering discards pixels based on a set pattern to smoothly fade without enabling transparency. On certain hardware, this can be faster than [constant DISTANCE_FADE_PIXEL_ALPHA].

constants.name: DISTANCE_FADE_OBJECT_DITHER
constants.summary: Smoothly fades the object out based on the object's distance from the camera using a dithering approach. Dithering discards pixels based on a set pattern to smoothly fade without enabling transparency. On certain hardware, this can be faster than [constant DISTANCE_FADE_PIXEL_ALPHA] and [constant DISTANCE_FADE_PIXEL_DITHER].

constants.name: STENCIL_MODE_DISABLED
constants.summary: Disables stencil operations.

constants.name: STENCIL_MODE_OUTLINE
constants.summary: Stencil preset which applies an outline to the object. [b]Note:[/b] Requires a [member Material.next_pass] material which will be automatically applied. Any manual changes made to [member Material.next_pass] will be lost when the stencil properties are modified or the scene is reloaded. To safely apply a [member Material.next_pass] material on a material that uses stencil presets, use [member GeometryInstance3D.material_overlay] instead.

constants.name: STENCIL_MODE_XRAY
constants.summary: Stencil preset which shows a silhouette of the object behind walls. [b]Note:[/b] Requires a [member Material.next_pass] material which will be automatically applied. Any manual changes made to [member Material.next_pass] will be lost when the stencil properties are modified or the scene is reloaded. To safely apply a [member Material.next_pass] material on a material that uses stencil presets, use [member GeometryInstance3D.material_overlay] instead.

constants.name: STENCIL_MODE_CUSTOM
constants.summary: Enables stencil operations without a preset.

constants.name: STENCIL_FLAG_READ
constants.summary: The material will only be rendered where it passes a stencil comparison with existing stencil buffer values.

constants.name: STENCIL_FLAG_WRITE
constants.summary: The material will write the reference value to the stencil buffer where it passes the depth test.

constants.name: STENCIL_FLAG_WRITE_DEPTH_FAIL
constants.summary: The material will write the reference value to the stencil buffer where it fails the depth test.

constants.name: STENCIL_COMPARE_ALWAYS
constants.summary: Always passes the stencil test.

constants.name: STENCIL_COMPARE_LESS
constants.summary: Passes the stencil test when the reference value is less than the existing stencil value.

constants.name: STENCIL_COMPARE_EQUAL
constants.summary: Passes the stencil test when the reference value is equal to the existing stencil value.

constants.name: STENCIL_COMPARE_LESS_OR_EQUAL
constants.summary: Passes the stencil test when the reference value is less than or equal to the existing stencil value.

constants.name: STENCIL_COMPARE_GREATER
constants.summary: Passes the stencil test when the reference value is greater than the existing stencil value.

constants.name: STENCIL_COMPARE_NOT_EQUAL
constants.summary: Passes the stencil test when the reference value is not equal to the existing stencil value.

constants.name: STENCIL_COMPARE_GREATER_OR_EQUAL
constants.summary: Passes the stencil test when the reference value is greater than or equal to the existing stencil value.
